title: Unit Final: Digital Citizenship in Practice

questions.prompt: What does applying digital citizenship skills mean?

choices.feedback: Digital citizenship is about how you use technology, not just when.

choices.text: Combining safety, responsibility, and respectful behavior in digital environments
choices.feedback: Correct. Applying digital citizenship means using multiple skills together.
Digital citizenship involves active and responsible participation.
Digital citizenship applies across all platforms.

questions.prompt: Real-world online scenarios often require multiple digital citizenship skills at the same time.

choices.feedback: Correct. Real situations often require combined skills.
Digital situations usually involve multiple skills together.

questions.prompt: A student is working on a group project online. What is the most important behavior?

choices.feedback: Collaboration requires communication.

choices.text: Taking over all tasks without input
choices.feedback: Collaboration involves shared responsibility.

choices.text: Communicating clearly and respectfully with team members
choices.feedback: Correct. Clear and respectful communication supports teamwork.
Effective teamwork requires full participation.

questions.prompt: Which action shows strong digital collaboration skills?

choices.text: Sharing ideas and listening to others’ perspectives
choices.feedback: Correct. Collaboration involves both sharing and listening.

choices.text: Working alone and not contributing to group discussions
choices.feedback: Collaboration requires participation.
Deadlines are important in teamwork.
This disrupts collaboration.

questions.prompt: Digital wellness includes managing screen time and maintaining a healthy balance.

choices.feedback: Correct. Digital wellness focuses on balance and well-being.
Managing screen time is part of digital wellness.

questions.prompt: Which example best demonstrates healthy technology habits?

choices.text: Using devices all night without breaks
choices.feedback: This can negatively affect health.

choices.text: Balancing screen time with offline activities
choices.feedback: Correct. Balance is key to digital wellness.
Physical activity is important for balance.

choices.text: Using multiple devices at the same time constantly
choices.feedback: Overuse can reduce focus and well-being.

questions.prompt: A student encounters a complex online situation involving misinformation and conflict. What is the best approach?

choices.feedback: Impulsive reactions can worsen the situation.

choices.text: Use critical thinking, verify information, and respond respectfully
choices.feedback: Correct. Combining skills leads to better decisions.
Some situations require action.

choices.text: Share the information quickly before checking
choices.feedback: This can spread misinformation.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of creating a digital citizenship action plan?

choices.feedback: The goal is responsible use, not avoidance.

choices.text: To plan how to apply safe, ethical, and responsible digital behaviors
choices.feedback: Correct. An action plan helps guide responsible behavior.
This is not the purpose of an action plan.
Action plans should be based on responsible choices.

questions.prompt: Effective digital communication includes clarity, respect, and awareness of audience.

choices.feedback: Correct. Good communication considers tone and audience.
These are key elements of effective communication.

questions.prompt: Which behavior best demonstrates digital leadership?

choices.text: Encouraging respectful behavior and guiding others to make responsible choices online
choices.feedback: Correct. Digital leadership involves modeling and promoting positive behavior.
Leaders take action to support positive environments.
Leadership involves critical thinking and guidance.

choices.text: Focusing only on personal use of technology
choices.feedback: Leadership involves influencing others positively.
